applicability.summary: Contract is Not Applicable when the architecture contains NEITHER a BPM platform NOR a Document Management System (DMS).

scoping_rules.neither_bpm_nor_dms: All four LAA codes Not Applicable. Contract scored N/A overall.
scoping_rules.bpm_only: Apply LAA1, LAA2, LAA3. Mark LAA4 Not Applicable (no DMS in architecture).
scoping_rules.dms_only_no_workflow: Apply LAA1, LAA3, LAA4. Mark LAA2 Not Applicable (no BPM platform in architecture).
scoping_rules.dms_with_embedded_workflow: If the project uses the DMS's embedded workflow engine for non-trivial process orchestration (e.g., FileNet Process Engine, Athento workflow), treat as both BPM and DMS — apply all four LAAs.

applicability.verification: Validator MUST grep ARCHITECTURE.md (Sections 4 Meta Architecture, 5 Component Model, 6 Data Model, 7 Integration View, 8 Technology Stack) for any BPM or DMS platform name and record the result in External Validation Summary under 'Applicability Verification'.

validation_sections.section_name: BPM / DMS Feasibility and Impact Analysis (LAA1) — applies if architecture includes a BPM platform (Camunda, IBM BAW, Pega, Activiti, Flowable, Bonita, Appian, ProcessMaker) and/or DMS (FileNet, Athento, SharePoint, Documentum, Alfresco, OpenText, Nuxeo, OnBase). Otherwise N/A — see contract Applicability section.

validation_items.question: Is current manual workflow effort (BPM-replaced approvals/hand-offs) or manual document effort (DMS-replaced filing/classification/retrieval) quantified in FTE hours/week?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: Manual effort quantified with specific FTE hours/week
validation_rules.na_criteria: Net-new BPM/DMS deployment with no prior manual process
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Process mentioned but effort not quantified

validation_items.question: Is BPM process / DMS workflow complexity assessed (simple/moderate/complex) — for BPM by BPMN node count, gateway count, escalation paths; for DMS by document type taxonomy size, classification rules, retention schedule complexity?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: Complexity categorized for BPM (BPMN nodes/gateways/escalations) and/or DMS (document types/classification rules/retention schedule)
validation_rules.na_criteria: N/A — neither BPM nor DMS in architecture
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: BPM/DMS described but complexity unclear

validation_items.question: Is BPM / DMS ROI documented — for BPM with cycle-time reduction (e.g., 5d → 4h) and labor cost reduction per process instance; for DMS with storage cost savings, retrieval-time reduction, and compliance-risk reduction?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM cycle-time reduction quantified and/or DMS storage / retrieval / compliance-risk savings calculated with cost units
validation_rules.na_criteria: Regulatory mandate (legal hold capability, RM compliance) — ROI not required
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Benefits mentioned but not quantified

validation_items.question: Are BPM connectors and DMS integration points documented? For BPM: every Service Task / External Task / Send Task target with connector type (External Task, REST connector, JMS, BAW Service Flow). For DMS: every API consumer (CMIS, REST, SMB, IMAP, e-signature, scanner pipeline).

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM connector inventory and DMS API surface documented with target system names and protocols
validation_rules.na_criteria: Standalone BPM with no external calls AND/OR standalone DMS with manual upload only
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Integrations mentioned but BPM/DMS-specific systems not identified

validation_items.question: Are data flows mapped — for BPM, process variable flow across Service Tasks and message correlations; for DMS, document content + metadata flow from ingestion source to repository to downstream consumer?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM process variable flow and/or DMS document/metadata flow documented showing source → transformation → destination
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Data mentioned but flows unclear

validation_items.question: Are case-worker / document-handler workflow changes and BPM tasklist / DMS UI training requirements documented?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM tasklist surface (Camunda Tasklist, BAW Process Portal, Pega Case Worker UI, custom forms) and/or DMS UI (Workplace XT, Content Navigator, Athento client, SharePoint library, Alfresco Share) named, with training plan
validation_rules.na_criteria: Headless BPM (no human tasks) AND headless DMS (API-only ingestion)
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: BPM/DMS mentioned but participant impact unclear

validation_items.question: Are process-variable / document-content quality standards defined — BPM start-form validation rules and Service Task validators, DMS OCR confidence threshold, mandatory metadata properties, content-type validation, virus-scan rule?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM variable validation rules and/or DMS document quality gates (OCR confidence, mandatory properties, virus scan) defined
validation_rules.fail_criteria: Process variable / document quality not addressed
validation_rules.na_criteria: Trivial process with no variables AND/OR DMS-only with single ingestion path
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Quality mentioned but standards unclear

validation_items.question: Are BPM process variables and/or DMS Document Classes classified by sensitivity (public/internal/confidential/restricted) driving BPM variable encryption and DMS Object Store / Document Class ACLs / RM security?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM variables and/or DMS Document Classes classified with sensitivity levels and corresponding security controls
validation_rules.fail_criteria: Process variable / document classification not performed
validation_rules.na_criteria: N/A — neither BPM variables nor DMS documents in scope
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Security mentioned but BPM/DMS classification unclear

validation_sections.section_name: BPM / DMS Operational Factors (LAA2) — applies if architecture includes a BPM platform OR a DMS using its embedded workflow engine for non-trivial process orchestration. Mark Not Applicable for DMS-only projects with no orchestration layer.

validation_items.question: Is the BPM execution model classified per BPMN task (User Task with SLA, Service Task sync/async, External Task, Receive Task event-driven, Timer Boundary) and/or the DMS ingestion path (manual upload, scan-to-DMS, email-to-DMS, API push) documented?
validation_items.architecture_md_section: Section 5 (Component Model) or Section 10 (Non-Functional Requirements) or Section 11 (Operational Considerations)

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM task types labeled with execution semantics and human-task SLAs, and/or DMS ingestion path classified with availability target
validation_rules.fail_criteria: BPM execution model / DMS ingestion path not defined
validation_rules.na_criteria: N/A — no BPM platform AND no DMS workflow trigger
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: BPM/DMS mentioned but execution semantics unclear

validation_items.question: Are BPM time-critical SLAs and DMS ingestion latency targets specified (e.g., approval within 4 business hours; scanned document available within 2 minutes)?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM cycle-time SLA targets and DMS ingestion-to-availability latency documented
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Timing mentioned but BPM SLA / DMS latency unclear

validation_items.question: Are BPM process-instance / DMS document throughput and engine sizing documented — peak and steady-state instances/day, engine cluster topology, broker capacity, DMS storage projection?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM throughput (instances/day peak and steady-state, concurrent active instances) and engine sizing documented; DMS ingestion rate and year-3 storage projection documented
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Volume mentioned but BPM/DMS-specific numbers unclear

validation_items.question: Is BPM Incident handling / DMS error pipeline documented — Camunda Cockpit / BAW PA Incident triage, External Task retry, dead-letter policy, DMS failed-OCR queue, low-confidence-classification review queue, retention-policy failure alerts?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM Incident triage flow and/or DMS exception handling documented with retry/escalation policy
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Errors mentioned but BPM/DMS-specific handling unclear

validation_items.question: Are BPM and/or DMS platform license costs documented with platform-specific metrics — Camunda per-engine, IBM BAW PVU, Pega per case-type, Appian Application User, FileNet PVU + CAL + Connection license, Athento per-active-process + per-user + per-document-store, SharePoint per-server + CAL?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: Platform-specific licensing model and unit costs documented for the named BPM and/or DMS platform
validation_rules.na_criteria: Open-source-only stack (Camunda Community, Activiti Community, Alfresco Community)
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: BPM/DMS licensing mentioned but model and costs unclear

validation_items.question: Are BPM engine and DMS repository infrastructure costs estimated — engine pods/VMs, broker capacity, DMS object-store / file-system / DB tier, OCR pipeline?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM engine and DMS repository infrastructure costs estimated
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Resources mentioned but BPM/DMS-specific costs unclear

validation_items.question: Are BPM / DMS monitoring metrics defined — for BPM, per-model active instances, cycle time p50/p95, human-task SLA breaches, External Task fail rate, incidents; for DMS, ingestion success rate, classification confidence, retention-policy execution, repository free-space, OCR latency?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM and/or DMS dashboards defined with platform-specific metrics
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Monitoring mentioned but BPM/DMS-specific metrics unclear

validation_items.question: Is the BPM / DMS support model documented — L1 (tasklist user / retrieval issues), L2 (Incident triage, classification rule fixes, retention adjustments), L3 (engine upgrades, taxonomy / RM file-plan changes) with response-time SLAs?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: L1/L2/L3 BPM/DMS responsibilities and response-time SLAs defined
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Support mentioned but BPM/DMS-specific model unclear

validation_sections.section_name: BPM / DMS License Usage (LAA3) — applies if architecture includes a BPM platform and/or DMS. Otherwise N/A.

validation_items.question: Is the BPM / DMS license metric documented for the chosen platform — Camunda per-engine/per-cluster, IBM BAW PVU + Authorized User, Pega per case-type per environment + per developer seat, Appian Application User tier, FileNet PVU + Authorized User CAL + Connection, Athento per-active-process + per-user + per-repository, SharePoint per-server + CAL or M365 per-user, Documentum per-repository + Authorized User?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: Exact BPM/DMS license metric documented for the named platform
validation_rules.na_criteria: Open-source-only deployment (Camunda Community, Activiti, Alfresco Community)
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Licensing mentioned but BPM/DMS-specific metric unclear

validation_items.question: Is the required BPM / DMS license quantity calculated — engine count, PVU total, named-user CAL count, per-active-process count, repository / Object Store count, federation / connection license count?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM/DMS license quantity calculated based on platform metric (engines, PVU, CALs, repositories, federations)
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Licensing mentioned but BPM/DMS quantity unclear

validation_items.question: Are line-of-business integration licenses identified for systems the BPM engine calls or the DMS depends on — Salesforce per-API-call tier, SAP RFC connector, ServiceNow integration users, e-signature per-envelope (DocuSign / Adobe Sign), OCR engine (ABBYY, Tesseract Pro)?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: LOB integration licensing model documented per system the BPM Service Tasks call or the DMS pipeline depends on
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Integrations mentioned but licensing unclear

validation_items.question: Are BPM / DMS cost-reduction tactics documented — for BPM, cluster consolidation, tier-based engine sizing, off-peak External Task worker scaling, OSS for low-criticality flows; for DMS, storage tiering, Document-Class consolidation, federation to existing repositories, archive-tier migration?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM and/or DMS cost-optimization tactics documented
validation_rules.na_criteria: Fixed licensing with no optimization options
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Cost mentioned but BPM/DMS tactics unclear

validation_items.question: Is BPM / DMS license consumption tracked at the platform metric — engine count, PVU consumption, named-user CAL utilization, per-case-type counts, Authorized User CAL utilization, repository / Object Store count, federation usage — and reconciled against entitlements via vendor tooling (IBM ILMT, Flexera, vendor portals)?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: BPM/DMS license usage tracking and quarterly reconciliation procedures documented
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Monitoring mentioned but BPM/DMS approach unclear

validation_sections.section_name: Document Management Alignment (LAA4) — applies if architecture includes a DMS (FileNet, Athento, SharePoint, Documentum, Alfresco, OpenText Content Server, Nuxeo, OnBase, Box Governance, M-Files). Mark Not Applicable for BPM-only projects.

validation_items.question: Is the DMS-native object model and document lifecycle documented — for FileNet (Object Stores + Document Classes + Lifecycle Policies + Records Management states), Athento (Dossiers + Document Types + Workflow states + Retention Templates), SharePoint (Site Collections + Libraries + Content Types + Information Management Policies), Alfresco (Sites + Folders + Aspects + Rules)?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: DMS-native object model defined (Object Stores / Repositories, Document Classes / Content Types, lifecycle states from creation → classification → versioning → retention → disposition)
validation_rules.fail_criteria: DMS object model and lifecycle not specified
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: DMS mentioned but native model unclear

validation_items.question: Are DMS licenses documented with platform-specific metric — FileNet (PVU + Authorized User CAL + Connection / Federation + RM module), Athento (per-active-process + per-user + per-repository + premium connector), SharePoint (per-server + CAL or M365 per-user + AIP/Purview), Documentum (per-repository + Authorized User + xCP), Alfresco (per-repository + per-active-user + RM module), OpenText Content Server (per-user + connector), Nuxeo (per-CPU + per-active-user)?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: DMS license stack documented with platform-specific metrics and unit counts
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: DMS mentioned but licensing model unclear

validation_items.question: Is DMS authentication and authorization documented — authn via SAML / OAuth / Kerberos / AD-LDAP / service accounts; authorization via DMS-native ACLs (FileNet Object Store + Document Class ACLs, SharePoint permissions + groups, Alfresco group-based + Aspects, Athento dossier-level ACLs, Documentum ACL templates) plus RM record-class security?

validation_rules.pass_criteria: DMS authentication method AND DMS-native ACL model (Object Store / Library / Dossier ACLs, RM record-class security) documented
validation_rules.unknown_criteria: Security mentioned but DMS-native model unclear

8.0-10.0.message: High confidence validation. BPM / DMS Process Transformation contract automatically approved.

7.0-7.9.message: Validation passed with small gaps. Ready for Process Transformation Review Board review.

5.0-6.9.message: BPM / DMS Process Transformation analysis incomplete. Address gaps before approval.
blockers: Complete BPM/DMS feasibility (manual workflow / paper-document effort, BPM connector inventory, DMS API surface, BPMN/CMMN diagrams, DMS object model)
Document BPM/DMS operational factors (BPMN task types + SLAs, DMS ingestion paths, throughput, engine sizing, monitoring)
Address BPM/DMS license metrics, ELA alignment, and DMS records-management policy

0.0-4.9.message: Insufficient BPM / DMS Process Transformation documentation. Cannot proceed to review.
blockers: BPM/DMS feasibility not established (manual effort, BPMN/CMMN diagrams, DMS object model, integration inventory)
Critical BPM/DMS operational factors missing (execution model, throughput, engine sizing, monitoring)

remediation_guidance.applicability_check: If the architecture contains NEITHER a BPM platform NOR a DMS, mark all four LAA codes Not Applicable with the justification 'No BPM platform or DMS in architecture'. Score the contract N/A overall and exit the auto-approve gate.
remediation_guidance.FAIL_items: Document BPM/DMS feasibility (manual effort, BPMN/CMMN diagrams, DMS object model, BPM connectors, DMS API surface), operational factors (execution model, throughput, engine sizing, monitoring), license metrics (PVU, CAL, per-engine, per-active-process, federation), and DMS records-management policy in ARCHITECTURE.md
remediation_guidance.UNKNOWN_items: Add BPM/DMS-specific details to ARCHITECTURE.md Section 3 (Business Context — manual effort, ROI), Section 5 (Component Model — BPMN/CMMN diagrams, DMS components), Section 6 (Data Model — DMS object model, Document Classes, retention), Section 7 (Integration View — BPM connectors, DMS APIs), Section 8 (Technology Stack — BPM/DMS platform names, license metrics), Section 10 (NFRs — throughput, engine sizing), Section 11 (Operational Considerations — BPM execution model, DMS workflow, monitoring, retention)
remediation_guidance.completeness_below_threshold: Complete all applicable LAA requirements: feasibility analysis (LAA1), BPM/DMS operational factors (LAA2), license efficiency (LAA3), and document management alignment (LAA4). LAA4 is N/A for BPM-only projects.
remediation_guidance.low_quality_score: Improve traceability by referencing specific ARCHITECTURE.md sections and naming the exact BPM platform (Camunda, IBM BAW, Pega, etc.) and/or DMS (FileNet, Athento, SharePoint, etc.)
